title: Shadows of Brimstone - Part 26 - Hexcrawl and Campaign Stuff - Part 1
body:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=J-aiAO8-Czk

---

Daniel (@dungeondive) introduces the hex crawl fan expansion for Shadows of Brimstone — a free download from BGG that he considers the single best fan-made expansion for any game, and one he refuses to play without.

Created by Graham Henson (Pattern on BGG) and Sid Rain, hex crawl adds an overland map of the Brimstone region with mines, frontier settlements, forts, and varied terrain. Heroes travel between locations using movement points (six per day on horseback), rolling for wilderness encounters each morning. The expansion introduces persistent towns where you track locations, bounties, jobs, and destruction — creating a living world that persists across your entire campaign.

The jobs board offers a d100 table of side quests that can escalate into full missions. A mine tracker monitors the state of all 20 mine locations. New wilderness and terrain encounters replace travel hazards with location-appropriate events. Hero keywords like Scout, Science, and Performer unlock special overland actions.

Daniel printed the entire expansion spiral-bound for about eight dollars and keeps enemy cards as PDFs on his Kindle. He's so convinced of its quality that he uses it even with Forbidden Fortress content, simply treating eastern worlds as portals that have opened in the Old West. The expansion transforms Shadows of Brimstone from a good dungeon crawl into what Daniel calls a masterpiece-level game.
